sourceSummary: Nora Iyer uploaded protocol notes for a JoVE Journal manuscript. The AI draft used JoVE section rules, subject terminology, and published-article examples for Environmental Sciences.
styleGuideContext: All required JoVE sections must be present.
Protocol instructions use numbered imperative voice.
No first-person phrasing in restricted manuscript sections.
Figures, tables, materials, and safety notes must be explicitly referenced.

sections.title: Introduction
sections.body: This section frames the environmental sciences problem, describes why the visual method matters, and positions the procedure for JoVE viewers. The draft summarizes the scientific rationale, expected learning outcome, and experimental context for reproducible execution.

sections.title: Protocol
sections.body: 1. Prepare samples, reagents, and equipment, including the fluorescence microscope. 2. Label each sample and control before beginning the procedure. 3. Execute the procedure in numbered imperative steps. 4. Record timing, temperature, and visual acceptance criteria after each critical step.

sections.title: Representative Results
sections.body: Representative outputs should show the expected visual endpoint, quantified comparison, and figure references. The draft calls out Figure 1 for setup, Figure 2 for the observed outcome, and Table 1 for quality control values.

sections.title: Discussion
sections.body: The discussion identifies critical steps, troubleshooting guidance, limitations, and safe handling notes. It avoids first-person phrasing and keeps claims tied to observations captured during filming.

validationIssues.title: Representative results need tighter figure references
validationIssues.recommendation: Attach figure labels to each expected visual outcome before editorial review.

validationIssues.title: JoVE section structure preserved
validationIssues.recommendation: Locked sections are present and cannot be deleted in the author editor.

editorialNotes: AI draft generated with JoVE house structure locked.
Ready for editor approval or light copy edit.
